seo.title_value: DocTalk — Chat with your PDF documents
seo.title_verdict: Good — under 60 chars, keyword 'PDF' included, brand name leads
seo.meta_description_value: Upload a PDF and ask questions about it. Get instant answers from your documents with AI-powered chat.
seo.meta_description_verdict: Below optimal range (120-160 chars). Should be expanded for better SERP click-through.
seo.og_verdict: Partial — og:title and og:description present but og:image, og:url, and Twitter Card meta are missing. Social shares will have no preview image.
seo.structured_data_verdict: No JSON-LD found. A WebApplication or SoftwareApplication schema would improve search appearance.

core_web_vitals.font_strategy: Optimal — next/font/google with Geist + Geist_Mono, latin subset, CSS variable injection. Zero render-blocking font requests, automatic font-display:swap.
core_web_vitals.image_optimization: N/A — no images used in the app. The SVG illustration in EmptyState is inline. If images are added later, next/image should be used.
core_web_vitals.css_strategy: Good — Tailwind v4 via @import with PostCSS plugin. Single 23KB CSS bundle. No render-blocking external stylesheets. Custom properties defined in @theme inline block.
bundle_concerns: framer-motion is the largest dependency at ~223KB chunk. For a 2-page app this is acceptable, but if bundle size becomes a concern, consider framer-motion/m for reduced bundle or motion-lite alternatives.
@phosphor-icons/react imports individual icons (CloudArrowUpIcon, ArrowRightIcon, etc.) — correct tree-shaking pattern, no barrel import issue.

Both pages are 'use client' — entire page trees are client-rendered. The upload page could have a server component wrapper with the interactive drop-zone as a nested client component, but for this app scale the impact is negligible.
No dynamic imports (next/dynamic) used. At 979KB total static assets, this is fine — no lazy-loading needed at this scale.
core_web_vitals.estimated_lcp: Good — LCP element is the h1 text headline on upload page and the chat header on chat page. No large images or heavy above-fold assets.
cls_risks: Error banner on upload page animates in below the drop zone, pushing content down. Minor CLS risk. Fix: use absolute/fixed positioning or reserve space.
Chat EmptyState disappears when first message arrives — layout reflow is unavoidable but the motion transition smooths it.

recommendations: framer-motion is 223KB — acceptable for current scope but monitor if more pages are added. Consider lazy-loading AnimatePresence on chat page via next/dynamic if needed.
Total 979KB is reasonable for a 2-page SPA with animations. No immediate action required.
All pages are statically generated (○ marker in build output) — good for initial load performance.

accessibility_seo.semantic_html: Good — upload page uses <main>, chat page uses <header> with <h1>, message area uses role='log'. Proper landmark structure.
accessibility_seo.heading_hierarchy: Upload page: single <h1> 'Talk to your documents' — correct. Chat page: <h1> is the filename in the header — correct. EmptyState has <h2> 'Ask anything about this document' — proper hierarchy under the page h1.
accessibility_seo.landmarks: Upload page: <main> wraps content. Chat page: <header> for nav bar, implicit <main> via flex layout, role='log' for message area. Chat input has sr-only <label>.
aria_coverage: Drop zone: aria-label, aria-disabled, keyboard accessible (Enter/Space)
File input: hidden with aria-hidden, drop zone serves as visual label
Send button: dynamic aria-label (changes when streaming)
Streaming state: sr-only 'AI is responding...' announcement
Chat textarea: sr-only <label> 'Ask a question about your document'
Back link: aria-label for screen readers
issues: Starter chips in EmptyState lack role='listbox' or similar grouping — minor, text content is self-descriptive

recommendations.fix:
Create frontend/public/robots.txt with: User-agent: *
Allow: /
Disallow: /api/
Sitemap: https://doctalk.app/sitemap.xml (adjust domain). Without this, crawlers may index API routes or miss the sitemap.
Add a static frontend/public/sitemap.xml or use Next.js app/sitemap.ts to generate one. For a 2-page app: list / and /chat (though /chat requires query params so it may be excluded).

Add WebApplication JSON-LD schema in layout.tsx: { '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'WebApplication', name: 'DocTalk', description: '...', applicationCategory: 'UtilityApplication', operatingSystem: 'Any' }. This improves rich snippet eligibility.

recommendations.issue: Missing og:image — social shares will have no preview image
recommendations.fix: Create a 1200x630 OG image (e.g., public/og-image.png) and add to metadata: openGraph: { images: [{ url: '/og-image.png', width: 1200, height: 630 }] }. Also add og:url and Twitter Card meta (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image).

recommendations.issue: Meta description is 97 chars — below optimal 120-160 char range
recommendations.fix: Expand to ~150 chars. Suggested: 'Upload a PDF and ask questions about it. Get instant, accurate answers from your documents through a simple chat interface. No signup required.' (147 chars)

recommendations.issue: Meta description contains 'AI-powered' which may be in voice charter forbidden phrases
recommendations.fix: Replace 'AI-powered chat' with 'a simple chat interface' or 'conversational search' to match the voice charter.

Add metadataBase and canonical in layout.tsx metadata: metadataBase: new URL('https://doctalk.app'), alternates: { canonical: '/' }. Prevents duplicate content issues across www/non-www or trailing slashes.

recommendations.issue: Error banner causes minor CLS on upload page
recommendations.fix: Use min-height reservation or position the error as an absolute overlay/toast instead of pushing content down.

recommendations.issue: framer-motion is the largest bundle chunk at 223KB
recommendations.fix: Acceptable for current 2-page scope. If bundle size becomes a concern, consider: (1) import { m } from 'framer-motion' for reduced features, (2) lazy-load AnimatePresence via next/dynamic on chat page, or (3) evaluate motion-one as a lighter alternative (~5KB).

recommendations.issue: Both pages are fully client-rendered ('use client' at top level)
recommendations.fix: For a 2-page app this is fine. If the app grows, consider extracting static layout sections (header, footer) as server components and wrapping only interactive parts in client boundaries.

recommendations.issue: No favicon or app icons configured beyond default
recommendations.fix: Add apple-touch-icon, favicon-32x32, favicon-16x16 in the public/ directory and reference via metadata.icons in layout.tsx for better mobile bookmark appearance.

report: DocTalk passes the SEO audit with warnings. Core strengths: excellent font strategy (next/font with zero render-blocking), good semantic HTML with comprehensive ARIA coverage, reasonable bundle size (979KB total, 23KB CSS), and all pages statically generated. The three critical gaps are: no robots.txt (crawlers have no guidance), no sitemap.xml (discovery issue), and no structured data (missed rich snippet opportunity). Additionally, og:image is missing which means social shares will render without a preview image, and the meta description at 97 chars is below the optimal 120-160 range. Bundle-wise, framer-motion at 223KB is the largest dependency but acceptable for this app's scope. No performance-critical issues were found — all recommendations are SEO completeness items rather than runtime bottlenecks.
